mechanism_narrative: A sustained low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates tight labor market conditions, which generate upward pressure on wages and inflation expectations, causing nominal 10-year Treasury yields to rise over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: any regime where UNRATE is below the estimated NAIRU of 4.5%
failure_condition: If 20-day forward yield change is negative in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE <= 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth exceeding 3% signals persistent price pressures above the Federal Reserve's 2% target, leading to expectations of policy tightening and an upward repricing of the 10-year Treasury yield.
failure_condition: If the trigger condition holds but 20-day forward yield change is negative more than 60% of the time, or the average yield change is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and real retail sales (RSAFS) jointly indicate robust aggregate demand expansion, which raises real GDP growth expectations and puts upward pressure on 10-year Treasury yields.
failure_condition: If over a 3-year rolling window the joint signal (both proxies increasing) does not predict positive yield changes significantly better than a single proxy alone, as measured by improved out-of-sample R².

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a rising federal funds rate directly lifts short-term rates and increases term premia, which transmit to higher long-term yields, particularly the 10-year Treasury.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FOMC raising target range or signaling further hikes)
failure_condition: If in instances identified as tightening cycles, the 20-day yield change is negative more than 50% of the time, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS change and yield change is negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index (INDPRO) signals strong factory output and productive capacity expansion, which boosts real economic growth expectations and consequently increases the equilibrium real rate and nominal 10-year Treasury yields.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward yield change is negative on average when INDPRO increases month-over-month, or if the Rank IC over a 5-day horizon is below 0.03 for any year.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above the 1300 thousand level signal strong housing demand and construction activity, which stimulate durable goods consumption and raise GDP growth expectations, pushing up 10-year yields.
failure_condition: If when HOUST > 1300, the 20-day forward yield change is negative more than 60% of the time, or the average yield change is not positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) register consecutive monthly increases, it indicates a self-reinforcing cycle of labor income and consumer spending that strongly lifts near-term growth expectations and drives 10-year yields higher.
failure_condition: If in periods where both series increase month-over-month, the 20-day yield change is negative on average, or the joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals in predicting yield direction.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation economic environment, rising unemployment (UNRATE) triggers fears of stagflation, prompting a flight to safety into longer-term Treasuries which compresses yields downward, opposite to the usual yield-increasing effect of low unemployment.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points from previous month
regime_hint: high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%) AND rising unemployment
failure_condition: If when triggered, 20-day forward yield change is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average yield change is not negative and significant.
